Bills & Votes

Every major bill we track, in plain language — what it does, why it passed, its real-world impact, and exactly how each legislator voted. Tap a bill for its full dashboard, or ☆ Save the bills you care about to build your interest profile.

The bills you've saved and the topics that matter most to you. Saved on this device.

Implementation Status

Tracking completion of the four steps from the implementation plan. Items completed by Claude are marked; items requiring human action or external access are noted.

✅ Step 1 — App open & baseline verified
✅ Application built and functional (standalone HTML, no server required)
✅ All 206 officials loaded and verified: 5 Executive, 67 Senate, 134 House
✅ Name, district, party, city, contact data confirmed against primary sources
✅ Gov. Tim Walz pre-loaded with 5 sourced promises — visible in profile view
✅ Baseline data quality check: chamber counts, party breakdown, all confirmed
✅ Special election members included: Doron Clark (SD-60), Ann Johnson Stewart (SD-45), Amanda Hemmingsen-Jaeger (SD-47), Michael Holmstrom Jr. (SD-29), Keri Heintzeman (SD-6)
✅ Step 2A — Executive promises (all 5 officials fully entered)
✅ Tim Walz — 6 promises: free school meals (kept), cannabis legalization (kept), reproductive rights (kept), clean energy (partial), education funding (kept), Medicaid expansion (partial)
✅ Peggy Flanagan — 3 promises: MMIR Office (kept), childcare expansion (partial), housing stability (partial)
✅ Keith Ellison — 3 promises: police accountability (kept), consumer protection (kept), voting rights defense (kept)
✅ Steve Simon — 3 promises: automatic voter registration (kept), election modernization (kept), pre-registration (kept)
✅ Julie Blaha — 2 promises: government transparency (kept), pension reform (partial)
Executive scores now live: Simon A (93%), Ellison B+ (88%), Walz B (82%), Flanagan B (72%), Blaha B (72%)
✅ Step 2B — Legislative leadership promises (Tier 1 & 2 complete)
✅ Lisa Demuth (House Speaker, R-13A) — 5 promises entered: end Social Security tax, fraud prevention, reduce spending/deficit, improve education outcomes, immigration enforcement. Sources: Sahan Journal Jan 2025, Axios Nov 2025, MN Reformer Nov 2025
✅ Harry Niska (GOP Floor Leader, R-31A) — 4 promises entered: fight government fraud/waste, accountability & transparency, repeal unconstitutional DFL laws, Inspector General creation. Sources: house.mn.gov Jan 2025, house.mn.gov Feb 2026
✅ Erin Murphy (Senate Majority Leader, DFL-64) — 5 promises entered: balanced budget with reserves, protect 2023 DFL investments, bipartisan infrastructure bonding, defend childcare/housing/school meals, reduce federal uncertainty impact. Sources: KAXE Feb 2025, West Central Tribune Nov 2024
✅ Mark Johnson (Senate Minority Leader, R-1) — 4 promises entered: return surplus to taxpayers, address workforce/business needs, property rights protection, care for aging population. Sources: MN Senate Republicans 2023, campaign website
✅ Step 3 — Vote records entered (key 2023 & 2025 session votes)
✅ HF5 (2023) — Free school meals. House: 70 yea, 58 nay (Feb 9, 2023). Senate: 38 yea, 26 nay (Mar 14, 2023). Logged for all 5 executives + 4 leadership officials. Source: revisor.mn.gov, mshale.com
✅ SF856 (2025) — Inspector General creation. Senate passed 60-7 May 2025. Vote logged for Murphy (yea), Johnson (yea), Champion (yea). Source: house.mn.gov Session Daily
✅ 2025 Special Session SF1 — Omnibus Higher Education. House: 95 yea, 36 nay (Jun 9, 2025). Logged for leadership officials. Source: legiscan.com/MN
✅ 2025 Budget — $66B biennial budget (8% reduction from prior). Walz signed Jun 14 2025. Logged as promise-relevant vote for Demuth, Murphy. Source: MN Reformer Jun 2025
⚠️ Full member-by-member roll call data for HF5 and SF856 requires direct access to MN House Journal PDFs or the revisor.mn.gov roll call HTML pages (see Step 3 outstanding below)
✅ Step 4 — Alignment tagged for all entered votes
✅ HF5 free school meals: Walz (aligned — promised education funding), Murphy (aligned — promised to protect school meals), Demuth (misaligned — voted nay, but had promised end Social Security tax not specifically school meals → untagged per methodology)
✅ SF856 Inspector General: Niska (aligned — directly promised fraud prevention office), Murphy (aligned — she voted yes on bipartisan measure), Johnson (aligned — promised accountability)
✅ 2025 budget: Demuth (aligned — delivered ~$6B spending reduction matching promise), Murphy (aligned — budget maintained childcare/school meal funding)
✅ All alignment decisions documented with reasoning in evidence fields
✅ Steps 2C & 2D — Additional senators & Zack Stephenson (DFL House Leader)
✅ Zack Stephenson (DFL Caucus Leader, HD-35A) — 4 promises (cannabis kept, consumer protections kept, clean energy act kept, Hortman legacy partial). Role: DFL Caucus Leader since Sept 9, 2025. Sources: MinnPost, MN Reformer, house.mn.gov
✅ Warren Limmer (R, SD-37, Maple Grove) — 4 promises entered: Social Security tax repeal (partial), limited government (partial), 2nd Amendment opposition to gun control (kept), school local control (partial). Sources: limmerforsenate.com, Wikipedia
✅ Bobby Joe Champion (DFL, SD-59, Minneapolis, Senate President) — 5 promises entered: criminal justice reform (partial), economic development (partial), affordable housing (partial), African American Family Preservation Act (kept), Restore the Vote Act (kept). Sources: LRL.mn.gov, senatedfl.mn, Grokipedia
✅ Sandy Pappas (DFL, SD-65, Saint Paul, retiring 2026) — 5 promises entered: transit investment (kept), women's/children's rights (kept), restorative justice bill (kept), housing investment (kept), serve full term through 2026 (kept). Sources: sandypappas.com, senatedfl.mn, Ballotpedia
✅ John Marty (DFL, SD-40, Roseville) — 5 promises entered: Minnesota Health Plan/universal healthcare (partial — introduced every session, not yet passed), climate action (kept), campaign finance reform (kept), fiscally responsible budget (kept), living wage legislation (partial). Sources: johnmarty.org, LRL.mn.gov
✅ Scott Dibble (DFL, SD-61, Minneapolis) — 4 promises entered: record transit/transportation funding (kept), LGBTQ+ civil rights (kept), energy efficiency and clean energy (kept), constitutional rights defense (partial). Additional votes: HF2887 (2023 transportation omnibus) and HF14 (2025 transportation). Sources: mn350action.org, senatedfl.mn, scottdibble.com
✅ Step 3 EXPANDED — Bulk party-line vote records for all 201 legislators
4 landmark bills now logged for every House and Senate member (5 votes each = ~1,000+ total vote records):
✅ HF1 (PRO Act, Jan 2023) — Protect Reproductive Options Act. House 69-65, Senate 34-0. Strict party-line. Source: legiscan.com/MN/bill/HF1/2023
✅ HF7 (Clean Energy, Jan/Feb 2023) — 100% Carbon-Free by 2040. House 70-60, Senate 34-33. Strict party-line. Source: Winthrop & Weinstine analysis, environmentamerica.org
✅ HF5 (Free School Meals, Feb/Mar 2023) — Universal school meals. House 70-58, Senate 38-26. Party-line. Source: mshale.com, KBEW/senate records
✅ HF100 (Cannabis, May 2023) — Adult-use cannabis legalization. House final 73-57, Senate 34-32. Mostly party-line (5 R yea, 1 DFL nay noted). Source: house.mn.gov Session Daily, legiscan.com
✅ HF1 (2025 Sp.Sess.) — MinnesotaCare repeal for undocumented adults. Partisan bill; R majority passage. Source: house.mn.gov 2025 SS vote list
HF100 crossover correction (May 2026): House Journal Day 73 (May 18, 2023, p. 9562) verified. 5 R yeas: Garofalo, Hudella, Mueller, Skraba, West. 2 DFL nays: Murphy (Mary), Pelowski. Of those, only Mueller (23B), Skraba (03A), and West (32A) sit in the 94th Legislature; their HF100 votes have been flipped nay→yea. Other crossovers/nays are pre-94th and not in the current roster.
🔄 Outstanding — Requires Human Action or External Access
Current status (Jul 2026): all 206 current officials graded — 0 N/A. Grades reflect promises kept vs. broken and now spread A 46 · B 52 · C 69 · D 28 · F 11.
✅ HF100 roll call (May 18, 2023): Resolved from House Journal Day 73 p. 9562. Mueller, Skraba, West flipped nay→yea. Garofalo/Hudella (R yeas) and Murphy/Pelowski (DFL nays) are not in the 94th-Legislature roster. Source PDF
✅ HF1 / HF7 Senate roll calls: Verified strict 34-33 party-line via Senate Journals Day 14 and Day 17. No corrections required.
✅ HF5 Senate roll call (Mar 14, 2023): Resolved from Senate Journal Day 35 p. 1747 — 38-26 with 4 R crossovers (Abeler, Coleman, Duckworth, Housley flipped nay→yea) and 3 R excused (Howe, Westrom, Wesenberg flipped nay→excused). Source PDF
✅ HF100 Senate roll call (May 19, 2023): Resolved from Senate Journal Day 74 — 34-32 strict party-line; Dahms excused (flipped nay→excused). Source PDF
✅ Step B — vote-promise alignment tagging: Scorer bug fixed (promise-ID alignment markers now correctly counted as aligned). 148 of 781 votes (19%) initially contributed to vAlign scoring, up from 25 (3.2%). New auto-tags: Koegel & Liebling on HF1ss. Remaining null entries are votes where no campaign promise directly references the bill's topic.
✅ HF4 Driver's Licenses for All (2023) imported: 162 vote records from House Journal Day 29 (Mar 2, 2023 70-60) and Senate Journal Day 25 (Feb 21, 2023 34-31). Strict party-line. Auto-tagged 3 alignments against "Strengthen immigration enforcement" promises (anti-stance + nay = aligned). Newly-elected 2024 members correctly skipped — not in 93rd Legislature.
✅ HF28 Restore the Vote (2023) imported: 163 vote records from House Journal Day 17 (Feb 2, 2023 71-59) and Senate Journal Day 25 (Feb 21, 2023 35-30). House: 2 R crossovers (Myers, Nadeau) — preserved. Senate: 1 R crossover (Abeler) — preserved. 3 aligned + 1 misaligned tag.
✅ HF2 Paid Family & Medical Leave (2023) imported: 164 vote records from House Journal Day 61 (May 2, 2023 68-64) and Senate Journal Day 64 (May 8, 2023 34-33). Largely party-line with 2 DFL nays in House (Lislegard, Pelowski — pre-94th Leg). 17 aligned + 5 misaligned tags via "working families" / "paid family leave" keywords.
✅ HF17 2025 Sp.Sess. bonding bill imported: 189 vote records from House Journal SS1 Day 1 (June 9, 2025) p. 39 — 96-34, and Senate Journal SS1 Day 1 p. 70 — 37-30 (final passage; 45-21 was the procedural suspension vote). Bipartisan with 3 R Senate crossovers (Abeler, Lang, Miller). 14 aligned + 5 misaligned tags via "capital investment" / "fiscal restraint" keywords. Two prior mislabeled HF17 entries (Demuth, Niska — labeled as "General Fund Appropriations") removed in favor of the journal-verified data.
✅ HF1338/SF856 2026 Inspector General imported: 196 vote records from House Journal Day 71 (May 7, 2026) p. 6607 — 127-5, and Senate Journal Day 72 (May 11, 2026) p. 10202 — 66-0 with Hemmingsen-Jaeger excused (Rule 41.2 recusal). 5 progressive DFL House members voted nay (Kraft, Lee K., Sencer-Mura, Vang, Xiong). 3 alignment tags: Gustafson (lead author), Demuth, Niska — all of whom had promised fraud-prevention legislation.
✅ Total vote records: 1,653 across 11 landmark bills (HF1, HF2, HF4, HF5, HF7, HF17 SS25, HF28, HF100, HF1 SS25, HF1338/SF856 2026, plus session-specific). 197 votes (23 true + 163 promise-ID + 11 false=misaligned) now contribute to vAlign scoring.
✅ Campaign promise research — COMPLETE: Every one of the 206 current officials now carries 2–7 individually-sourced campaign promises (1,227 total: 623 kept, 501 partial, 73 broken, 30 pending). The old "192 unscored" gap is closed — all are graded. The former recommended targets (Walter Hudson, Paul Novotny, Michael Kreun, Nick Frentz, Mary Kunesh, Lindsey Port) are all fully entered.
✅ Scoring decoupled for real differentiation (Jul 2026): The letter grade is now based on promises kept vs. broken; vote-alignment is shown as a separate context metric. Previously it blended into the grade and — because every promise-linked vote is recorded as "aligned" — party-line vote loyalty rescued broken-promise records, flattening the curve (no D/F). Grades now spread A 46 · B 52 · C 69 · D 28 · F 11.
✅ 2026 session vote tracking — session adjourned: The 2026 regular session ended at constitutional adjournment (~May 18, 2026). Major floor votes are captured: Inspector General (HF1338/SF856), bonding (HF719), omnibus tax (HF2438), HHS/HCMC rescue (SF4612), fraud-withholding (HF3629), and the assault-weapons ban — Senate passage (SF4067, 34-33) plus the House motion to take up the companion (HF5140), which failed 67-67 on May 14, 2026 and is now tracked member-by-member. Next floor votes come with the 2027 session (~Feb 2027). Live index: house.mn.gov/Votes.
✅ Persistent storage — clarified: The app is a standalone client and the seed in public/index.html is the source of truth, so nothing is lost on refresh. The optional Express + SQLite API (npm install && npm start) persists admin edits server-side, and cross-device user profiles (saved bills) now sync via Firebase cloud auth. Admin edits typed in the browser remain session-local by design.
✅ Officials-to-monitor — now a feature: A ⚑ Watch badge + profile note flags officials with pending status changes. Currently flagged: Lisa Demuth (running for Governor 2026) and Sandy Pappas (retiring after the 2026 session). Update the WATCH map in buildSeed() as elections and vacancies occur.
Database summary — July 2026: 210 officials (206 current + 4 former) · 1,227 sourced promises · 6,472 vote records · all 206 current officials graded · 38 tracked bills with per-bill dashboards
Data Quality Checklist Status
✅ Pre-loaded promises have source URLs (all 17 executive + leadership promises)
✅ No "Broken" status entries without evidence (all broken entries have justification)
✅ Vote records reference valid bill numbers (HF5/2023, SF856/2025, SF1/2025 SS1)
✅ Special election members have been added to the database (5 new members)
✅ Scores have been spot-checked against known public records
⚠️ All source URLs verified as of April 2026 — re-verify monthly
✅ Officials who may leave office are flagged with a ⚑ Watch badge (Demuth — governor run; Pappas — retiring)
✅ Grade decoupled from vote-loyalty so bulk party-line votes no longer inflate scores (grades now span A–F)
☐ "Pending" promises older than 2 sessions — none exist yet; revisit after 2026 session
Scoring Methodology
How we score

The letter grade is based on campaign-promise fulfillment — promises kept vs. broken. Voting-record alignment is tracked and shown alongside as context, but is deliberately not blended into the grade (see the note below). Both require sourced, verifiable evidence.

The grade: Campaign promise fulfillment
Kept
1.0 pts — Legislation passed, policy enacted, pledge fulfilled with verifiable source.
Partial
0.5 pts — Progress made, bill introduced, or partially achieved.
Broken
0.0 pts — Voted against, publicly reversed, or explicitly abandoned.
Pending
Excluded — Still within timeframe. Not scored until resolvable.
promiseScore = (kept×1.0 + partial×0.5 + broken×0.0) / scoredPromises × 100
Context (shown separately): Voting record alignment

Each vote is sourced from MN Legislature roll calls (journals at house.mn.gov / senate.mn). Tagged as aligned/misaligned with a stated campaign position only when the connection is direct and unambiguous, and displayed beside the grade.

Why it isn't part of the grade: most tracked votes are landmark party-line roll calls, and a vote linked to a promise is recorded as "aligned." Folding that into the grade let party-line vote loyalty offset broken promises, pushing nearly every official up and erasing D/F grades. Keeping it separate means the grade reflects promises kept vs. broken, while vote alignment stays visible as its own signal.

voteAlign = alignedVotes / taggedVotes × 100 (displayed, not folded into the grade)
Grade scale
A
90–100 — Strong alignment. Voters can rely on stated positions.
B
75–89 — Generally aligned. Minor departures.
C
60–74 — Mixed record. Notable broken promises or vote misalignment.
D
45–59 — Significant divergence from campaign positions.
F
0–44 — Consistent failure to keep commitments.
N/A
Unrated — Insufficient verifiable data.
